Title: Successful user login with valid credentials
Preconditions:
- User account exists in FirebaseAuth with valid credentials.
- Server and database connections are active.
Test Steps:
1. User invokes `login(username: "john_doe", password: "SecurePass123!")` on the `User` class.
2. `User` forwards credentials to `FirebaseAuth.getCurrentUser()`.
3. `FirebaseAuth` validates credentials and returns `true`.
4. `User` calls `Server.start()` to establish a connection.
5. `Server` initiates `Database.connect()`.
6. `User` receives confirmation of successful login.
Expected Result:
- `login()` returns `true`.
- Database connection is established.
- User session is active (verified via `getCurrentUser()`).

Title: Failed login with invalid credentials
Preconditions: - User account exists, but credentials are incorrect.
Test Steps:
1. User invokes `login(username: "john_doe", password: "WrongPass456")`.
2. `FirebaseAuth.getCurrentUser()` rejects credentials.
3. `User` receives `false` from `login()`.
Expected Result:
- `login()` returns `false`.
- No database connection is established.
- User session remains inactive.

Title: User logout functionality
Test Steps:
1. User invokes `logout()`.
2. `User` calls `Server.stop()` to terminate session.
3. `Server` calls `Database.disconnect()`.
Expected Result:
- `logout()` executes without errors.
- Database connection is closed.
- `getCurrentUser()` returns `null` (verified via `FirebaseAuth`).

Title: Admin adds a new product to the system
Preconditions:
- Admin is logged in.
- Database connection is active.
Test Data:
- Product name: `Wireless Headphones`
- Description: `Noise-cancelling, 30-hour battery`
- Price: `99.99`
Test Steps:
1. Admin invokes `Product.add(name: "Wireless Headphones", description: "Noise-cancelling, 30-hour battery")`.
2. `Product` validates input (non-empty name/description, valid price).
3. `Product` persists data to `Database` via `Server`.
4. Admin verifies product exists in the system.
Expected Result:
- Product is added to the database.
- `add()` returns without errors.
- Product is retrievable via `getProducts()` (assumed method).

Title: Admin removes an existing product
Preconditions:
- Product `Wireless Headphones` exists in the system.
- Admin is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. Admin invokes `Product.remove(name: "Wireless Headphones")`.
2. `Product` queries `Database` to confirm existence.
3. `Product` deletes the product and updates `Database`.
Expected Result:
- Product is removed from the database.
- `remove()` returns without errors.
- Product is no longer retrievable.

Title: Remove non-existent product (negative case)
Test Steps:
1. Admin invokes `Product.remove(name: "NonExistentProduct")`.
2. `Product` queries `Database` and finds no match.
Expected Result:
- `remove()` returns an error (e.g., `ProductNotFoundException`).
- Database remains unchanged.

Title: User adds an item to the cart
Preconditions:
- User is logged in.
- Product `Wireless Headphones` exists.
Test Steps:
1. User retrieves the item (assumed via `getProduct()`).
2. User invokes `Cart.add(item: Wireless Headphones)`.
3. `Cart` validates item existence and updates `items` list.
Expected Result:
- Item is added to `Cart.items`.
- `add()` returns without errors.

Title: User removes an item from the cart
Preconditions:
- Cart contains `Wireless Headphones`.
- User is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. User invokes `Cart.remove(item: Wireless Headphones)`.
2. `Cart` validates item existence in `items` list.
3. `Cart` removes the item.
Expected Result:
- Item is removed from `Cart.items`.
- `remove()` returns without errors.

Title: Remove non-existent item from cart (negative case)
Preconditions:
- Cart is empty.
- User is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. User invokes `Cart.remove(item: NonExistentItem)`.
2. `Cart` finds no match in `items` list.
Expected Result:
- `remove()` returns an error (e.g., `ItemNotFoundException`).
- Cart remains empty.

Title: Admin adds a menu item to a restaurant
Preconditions:
- Admin is logged in.
- Restaurant `Pizza Palace` exists.
Test Steps:
1. Admin invokes `Restaurant.addMenu(name: "Margherita Pizza", price: 12.99)`.
2. `Restaurant` validates input and persists to `Database`.
3. Admin verifies menu item exists.
Expected Result:
- Menu item is added to `Pizza Palace`.
- `addMenu()` returns without errors.

Title: Admin removes a menu item
Preconditions:
- Menu item `Margherita Pizza` exists in `Pizza Palace`.
- Admin is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. Admin invokes `Restaurant.removeMenu(name: "Margherita Pizza")`.
2. `Restaurant` validates existence and removes the item.
Expected Result:
- Menu item is removed.
- `removeMenu()` returns without errors.

Title: Verify FirebaseAuth user retrieval
Test Steps:
1. User invokes `FirebaseAuth.getCurrentUser()`.
2. `FirebaseAuth` returns user data (email, password).
Expected Result:
- `getCurrentUser()` returns user object with `email` and `password`.
- No errors occur.

Title: FirebaseAuth user creation
Test Steps:
1. User invokes `FirebaseAuth.createUser(email: "new_user@example.com", password: "NewPass789!")`.
2. `FirebaseAuth` creates the user and returns confirmation.
3. User verifies `getCurrentUser()` returns the new user.
Expected Result:
- User is created in FirebaseAuth.
- `createUser()` returns `true`.
- `getCurrentUser()` retrieves the new user.

Title: Server establishes database connection
Preconditions:
- Database server is running.
- Server is started.
Test Steps:
1. `Server` invokes `Database.connect()`.
2. `Database` establishes connection and returns success.
Expected Result:
- Connection is active (verified via `Database.connection` attribute).
- No errors occur.

Title: Empty product name during addition
Test Steps:
1. Admin invokes `Product.add(name: "", description: "Valid description", price: 19.99)`.
2. `Product` validates input and rejects empty name.
Expected Result:
- `add()` throws `InvalidInputException`.
- Product is not added to the database.

Title: Negative price for product
Test Steps:
1. Admin invokes `Product.add(name: "Invalid Product", price: -9.99)`.
2. `Product` rejects negative price.

Title: End-to-end user workflow: Login → Add to Cart → Checkout
Test Steps:
1. User logs in with valid credentials (`login()`).
2. User adds `Wireless Headphones` to cart (`Cart.add()`).
3. User logs out (`logout()`).
4. User logs back in and verifies cart persists (assumed persistence).
Expected Result:
- All steps complete without errors.
- Cart retains items after logout/login.
- Database connections are managed correctly.
